bpWarnings.message: Table ConDemoModLifecycle does not have an alternate key
Relation under EDT 'CustAccount' must be migrated to table relation
EDT relation found in field CustAccount; should be migrated to a regular table relation
Relation properties [RelationshipType, RelatedTableCardinality, Cardinality] for table relation 'CustTable' are not set
No field has been specified for the 'Title Field2' property
No label is given for the 'Label' property of field group Overview
The mandatory property DeveloperDocumentation is not specified
Field CustAccount is not a member of a field group

root_cause_hypothesis: Case passes build + golden. Two silent-failure defects in d365fo_file(action=modify) on tables. (1) add-relation accepts relationshipType/relationCardinality/relatedTableCardinality per its own parameter spec (which even documents defaults Association/ZeroMore/ExactlyOne) but drops them: the emitted AxTableRelation contains only Name/RelatedTable/Constraints, and xppbp then raises BPErrorTableRelationshipPropertiesCompleteness. modify-property cannot repair it (Relations/* is not in its supported property list), so a BP-complete relation is unreachable through the modify surface. (2) modify-field returns success for a call that carries no recognised mutation parameter: params {fieldName, mandatory:true} reported 'Field Description modified' while writing nothing (correct key is fieldMandatory). Unknown params should be rejected, or a no-op should not report success. Residual BP warnings are inherent to the case spec, not defects: 'exactly one index' forbids an alternate key, TitleField1=TitleField2=Description follows from step 10, and the CustAccount EDT relation is a standard-EDT artefact.
suggested_fix_area: src bridge table-modify handlers: (a) add-relation must serialise relationshipType/relationCardinality/relatedTableCardinality (apply the documented defaults when omitted); (b) modify-field must fail on an unrecognised/empty mutation param set instead of reporting success; (c) consider surfacing the parameter spec proactively rather than only in the missing-required-param error path.
